tavily.answer: The latest conflict in Chad, particularly in the Lake Chad region, has led to significant security challenges and humanitarian concerns. On May 10, 2026, a violent attack by Islamist militants killed at least 23 soldiers, including two generals, prompting condemnation from the Union of Christian Executives of Chad (UCCT). The UCCT highlighted the impact of terrorism and the additional security burden from the influx of refugees from Sudan. The year 2025 saw a record high in internal displacements caused by conflict and violence, with 32.3 million people displaced globally, surpassing those displaced by natural disasters. In the Democratic Republic of Congo, the Ebola outbreak in Ituri province is exacerbated by ongoing conflict and displacement, complicating the response efforts. The World Health Organization (WHO) has warned of a "catastrophic collision" between the disease and conflict, with aid workers facing difficulties due to poor road conditions, conflict, and weakened health systems.
